title: AdenoBuilder: modular assembly of recombinant adenovirus genomes from high-copy plasmids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"33bvq","text":"The diverse applications of mutant and recombinant adenoviruses demand facile methods for their generation. This protocol allows the user to rapidly assemble customized adenoviral genomes from high-copy plasmids in a two-step isothermal reaction. Following in vitro assembly, full-length genomes can be directly packaged in HEK293 cells. Crude preparations of infectious virus can be prepared in less than one week.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":257,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkmer","text":"Adenovirus cause generally mild respiratory tract infections which are self-limiting and generally asymptomatic despite virologic and serologic proof of infection. BSL-2 facilities, equipment, and operational practices are required for work involving infectious or potentially infectious materials. Contact your institutional biosafety office for additional information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
